**ITEP 303 – WEB AND DATABASE INTEGRATION** 

| Name/s:  | Group \# :  |
| :---- | :---- |
| Group Performance Task \# 4  | Date :  |
| Section:  | Flask Jinja Web Application with MySQL  Database and ML Integration |

**ITST 303 – WEB AND DATABASE INTEGRATION** 

**Group Performance Task \#4** 

Activity Title: **Flask Jinja Web Application with MySQL Database and Machine  Learning Integration** 

**Group Composition** 

Each group must consist of three (3) members only. 

**Activity Overview** 

Modern web applications increasingly integrate intelligent systems capable of making  predictions, classifications, and recommendations. By combining web frameworks,  databases, and machine learning models, developers can create dynamic and data-driven  systems that support decision-making. 

In this activity, students will design and develop a Flask-based web application using: 

![][image1]Python Flask Framework  

![][image2]Jinja Templating Engine  

![][image3]MySQL Database  

![][image4]Simple Machine Learning Model  

The system must allow users to input data through a web interface, store records in MySQL,  and use a trained machine learning model to generate predictions or classifications. 

The final system should demonstrate integration between: 

![][image5]Backend Processing    
![][image6]Database Management  

![][image7]Web Interface    
![][image8]Machine Learning Prediction  

**Learning Objectives** 

At the end of this activity, students should be able to:  
1\. Develop a Flask web application using Jinja templates.    
2\. Connect Flask with MySQL database.  

3\. Implement CRUD operations.    
4\. Train and integrate a simple machine learning model.    
5\. Perform prediction using user input.  

6\. Deploy and demonstrate a functional intelligent web system.  7\. Apply web and database integration concepts in a real-world application.  

**Suggested Project Topics** 

Groups may choose any simple real-world prediction/classification system such as: 

∙ Student Performance Prediction  

∙ Employee Salary Prediction  

∙ House Price Prediction  

∙ Diabetes Risk Prediction    
∙ Customer Churn Prediction  

∙ Loan Approval Prediction    
∙ Spam Email Classification    
∙ Movie Recommendation System  

∙ Crop Recommendation System  

∙ Tourism Visitor Prediction  

**System Requirements** 

Each group must develop a working system containing the following: 1\. Flask Web Application 

The system must use the Flask framework. 

Required Flask Components: 

![][image9]Routes  

![][image10]Templates    
![][image11]Forms  

![][image12]Database Connection  

![][image13]CRUD Operations    
![][image14]Machine Learning Prediction Route  

2\. Jinja Template Integration 

Use Jinja templating to display: 

![][image15]Dynamic database records    
Prediction results   
User forms    
Reports or analytics  

3\. MySQL Database Integration 

Use MySQL as the database. 

Database must contain at least: 

Two (2) related tables    
Foreign Key Relationship    
Insert, Update, Delete, View functionality  

Example: 

Users Table  

Prediction Records Table  

4\. Machine Learning Integration 

Groups must implement a simple machine learning model using: 

scikit-learn 

Pandas   
NumPy 

Machine Learning Requirements 

Each group must: 

Select one dataset    
Train at least one ML model  

Save trained model    
Integrate prediction into Flask  

Example Models: 

Logistic Regression    
Linear Regression  

Decision Tree    
Random Forest    
KNN  

Naive Bayes  

5\. Dataset Requirement  
Dataset may be downloaded from: 

UCI Machine Learning Repository Kaggle 

Google Dataset Search 

**Required System Modules** 

The system must include: 

A. Database Module 

Add Records  

Edit Records    
Delete Records    
View Records  

B. Machine Learning Module 

Prediction Form    
Model Output  

Store Prediction Result  

C. Dashboard Module 

Display database summary  

Show prediction history    
Analytics or simple charts  

**Technical Requirements** 

Groups must use: 

Python  

Flask    
Jinja2  

MySQL  

Scikit-learn    
HTML/CSS    
Bootstrap (Optional)  

**Project Deliverables** 

Each group must submit: 

1\. PowerPoint Presentation (Canva Link)  
2\. System Demonstration (1-2 minutes recorded video) 

Presentation Requirements 

Each group will present for 8–10minutes. 

Suggested Presentation Outline: 

1\. Introduction    
2\. Problem Statement    
3\. System Features  

4\. Database Design    
5\. Machine Learning Model    
6\. Flask \+ MySQL Integration  

7\. Live Demonstration  

8\. Results    
9\. Conclusion  

**Additional Instruction** 

Students may use the uploaded starter project file as a reference or development basis for  this activity. The provided Flask \+ MySQL sample project may serve as a guide for: 

Flask project structure    
MySQL database connection    
CRUD implementation  

Jinja template rendering    
Route organization  

Form handling    
Basic backend logic  

However, groups are required to **modify, expand, and enhance** the system by integrating  their own: 

Machine Learning Model    
Database Design  

User Interface    
Prediction Workflow    
Additional Features  

The final output must demonstrate **original improvements and customization** beyond the  provided template.  
Students are encouraged to analyze the existing codebase to understand how Flask interacts  with MySQL and how machine learning can be embedded into a web application  environment. 

Evaluation Rubric

| Criteria  | Excellent (5)  | Good (4)  | Fair (3)  | Poor (2)  Incomplete  Poor   implementation Not integrated  Missing  Poor interface  Incomplete  Weak   presentation  Poor   collaboration |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Flask Application  | Fully functional  and organized | Mostly   functional  | Minor issues  |  |
| Database   Integration | Strong relational  structure | Functional   database | Basic   integration |  |
| Machine Learning  Integration | Properly trained  and integrated | Works with  minor issues | Basic prediction  only  |  |
| CRUD   Functionality  | Complete CRUD  | Mostly   complete  | Limited CRUD  |  |
| UI/UX Design  | Professional and  responsive  | Clean design  | Basic layout  |  |
| Documentation  | Complete and   organized | Minor missing  sections | Basic   documentation  |  |
| Presentation  | Clear and   professional  | Good delivery  | Limited   explanation |  |
| Team   Collaboration | Balanced   participation | Minor   imbalance  | Uneven work  |  |
